---
title: Monocle pseudotime on the UBC lineage only
date: 2023-07-19
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To run Monocle on the subsetted UBC lineage and identify the progenitors of the non-homologous cells.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<human Aldinger software directory>/run_monocle_ubc_lineage.R`.

### Output

Output directory is at `<human Aldinger software directory>/20230719`.


## Results

::: {#fig-principal-points}

![](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230719/full_rl_principal_points.png)

UMAP of human RL-derived cells showing the principal points of the pseudotime trajectory.

:::

To get all the cells along the UBC lineage (from RL-VZ to Late UBCs), I used `choose_graph_segment` with Y_9 and Y_104 as the starting and ending nodes (@fig-principal-points; saved to `monocle_cds_ubc_lineage.rds`).

I then re-ran PCA (`preprocess_cds`) and UMAP (`reduce_dimension`) on the subsetted cells.

::: {#fig-trajectory}

![](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230719/trajectory_umaps.png)

Trajectory of human UBC lineage cells coloured by pseudotime (left), Hendrikse cell type (middle), and my post-integration cell types (right).

:::

After running pseudotime on the UBC lineage cells, it still only shows one trajectory for the UBC lineage. So instead of having the UBCs branch off into homologous and non-homologous UBCs (@fig-branch), we're seeing a linear trajectory where the non-homologous UBCs arise from the progenitors (@fig-linear).

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-branch
%%| fig-cap: Our hypothesis about the origins of the non-homologous UBCs.
flowchart TD
  A["RL-VZ"] --> B["RL-SVZ"]
  B --> C["(some prognitor)"]
  C --> D["homologous UBC"]
  C --> E["non-homologous UBC"]
```

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-linear
%%| fig-cap: What pseudotime seems to be showing about the origins of the non-homologous UBCs.
flowchart TD
  A["RL-VZ"] --> B["RL-SVZ"]
  B --> C["homologous UBC"]
  C --> D["non-homologous UBC"]
```
